label: Light / Noetics / Illumination
description: The convergence of light as metaphysical principle across traditions — from Suhrawardi's Illuminationism and Ficino's solar theology to the luminous body of Neoplatonic theurgy, the Man of Light in Iranian Sufism, and modern noetic/consciousness studies.
core_insight: Light is not merely a metaphor for knowledge — it is the ontological medium through which the daimon communicates, the soul ascends, and reality renders itself.
key_concepts: The Sun as image of the Good (Plato, Ficino)
The Man of Light / Shakhs Nurani
The Black Light (Nur-e Siyah) of the Essence

suhrawardi.key_idea: Reality is graded light; the soul knows by becoming light

ficino_de_sole.key_idea: The Sun is the visible image of God; light is the medium of daimonic action

corbin_man_of_light.key_idea: The celestial Guide appears as a person of light; the soul's journey is a movement from darkness to light

dionysius.key_idea: God as 'Father of lights'; the hierarchy of illumination

pgm_helios.key_idea: Systasis with Helios — establishing relationship with the solar daimon

alchemical_sun.key_idea: The Philosophers' Stone is a solar body; the work is the generation of light in matter

proclus_light.key_idea: The One as superessential light; the soul's vehicle as luminous

needs_sourcing: Arthur Zajonc — Catching the Light: The Entwined History of Light and Mind
David Bohm — Wholeness and the Implicate Order (on light as information)
Thomas Campbell — My Big TOB (consciousness as light-based)
Wolfgang Schivelbusch — Disenchanted Night (cultural history of light)
Hans Jonas — The Phenomenon of Life (noetic light in Gnosticism)
